steps.name: Lower the deterministic variable-text forms and narrow the unsupported gate to shuffle-only
steps.description: Land the variable-text lowering mechanism (ADR-010) and flip matrix rows 25-27 from reject to compile, GREENing the VT1 once-only acceptance scenarios (vt-once-sticky, vt-once-bare). Implement: (1) ContentSegment.variableText(mode:stages:) AST case + VariableTextMode enum {sequence,cycle,once}; (2) a variable-text parse rule in InkParser.scanBraceSegment recognising a brace body with a top-level '|' and no top-level ':', reading the leading marker (&->cycle, !->once, none->sequence), stripping it, and '|'-splitting the body into stages; (3) a new stateless VariableTextEmitter (Compiler/Codegen/) with ONE parametrized routine over (op, bound, appendEmptyStage) emitting the #f:5 dispatch container + named seqN-sI stage containers + shared seqN-end rejoin, mirroring ConditionalEmitter's named-container/-end-rejoin pattern; (4) a .variableText dispatch branch in RuntimeObjectEmitter.lowerBody parallel to the inline-conditional branch, threading line suffix + rest-of-body as the continuation; (5) narrow UnsupportedConstructDetector.variableTextConstruct to return a construct ONLY for the '~' shuffle marker (sequence/cycle/once return nil), preserving the inline-conditional ':' discriminator and all statement-level rejects. Then reconcile the two existing reject suites that RED on the gate change and the feature-reference prose. Per DISTILL upstream-issues U-2 (authoritative) and DESIGN DDD-5, the gate flips to shuffle-only in this slice, so sequence and cycle ALSO compile here; their BOUNDARY verification scenarios (clamp-at-last, modulo-wrap, mixed) remain slices 02/03 and stay .disabled.

VT1: vt-once-bare compiles natively and plays ['The corridor falls quiet.'] (bare form lowers as a plain sequence with a leading empty stage; text emits on the second visit), identical to the oracle
VT0 shuffle regression guard stays GREEN: a '{~a|b}' shuffle still rejects with a located, construct-named .unsupportedConstruct error and produces no story
S6 UnsupportedRejection: reject-seq, reject-cycle, reject-once are removed from rejectCorpus; reject-shuffle plus the statement-level constructs (thread/list/random/external) still reject

S5 FeatureReferenceConsistency: the three variable-text rows move from documentedUnsupported to documentedSupported (point them at the vt-once-sticky / vt-seq-three / vt-cycle-two family, which carry committed oracles and compile); shuffle/thread/list/random/external stay documentedUnsupported
docs/product/ink-feature-reference.md rows 25-27 change MUST-REJECT -> MUST-COMPILE and the surrounding prose is updated to match (S5 suite is the SSOT the prose mirrors)
Both VT1 ATs have their .disabled trait removed (zero .disabled VT1 ATs remain for slice 01)
The full pre-existing suite stays GREEN (288 baseline at DISTILL hand-off, now +2 enabled VT1 scenarios); no regression in any Compiler_S* suite
Compiler-only diff: no change under Engine/, Decoder/, or Facade/; SwiftLint --strict R1/R3/R5 boundary rules pass (Compiler/ imports no Engine/, calls no JSONSerialization)

steps.scenario_name: US-01 — Compile and play a once-only variable-text form ({!a|b} / {|x|})
quality_gates: Both VT1 OnceOnly acceptance tests pass (native == oracle)
VT0 shuffle reject guard remains GREEN
Compiler_S5 and Compiler_S6 suites pass after their reject->supported reconciliation
Full swift test suite GREEN (no regressions)
SwiftLint --strict --no-cache passes (architecture-boundary R1/R3/R5)
implementation_notes: Lowering spec (DESIGN ground truth, verified vs inklecate): per form (OP, BOUND, append-empty): sequence = (MIN, S-1, no); cycle = (%, S, no); once = (MIN, S [new last index after appending], yes-1). Dispatch body: `ev visit BOUND OP /ev`, then per stage `ev du I == /ev {"->":"seqN-sI","c":true}`, then `nop`. Stage container seqN-sI = [pop, <^text if non-empty>, {"->":"seqN-end"}]; an empty stage omits the ^text node. seqN-end holds the continuation (line suffix + rest-of-body).

Worked examples: {!The lock clicks open.|} -> strip '!' -> '|'-split ['The lock clicks open.', ''] -> append empty -> ['The lock clicks open.','',''], visit 2 MIN (emits stage0 on visit0, blank after). Bare {|The corridor falls quiet.|} -> no marker -> sequence -> '|'-split ['','The corridor falls quiet.',''], visit 2 MIN (emits on visit1).

Mirror ConditionalEmitter exactly for boundary mechanics: nextOrdinal-style stable keys, key()/path() helpers, BranchLowerer/ExpressionLowerer typealiases, registering containers into the inout named collector, #f flag = 5 on the dispatch container (Visits | CountStartOnly so `visit` yields the 0-based own read count).

Use a DISTINCT key prefix (seq{N}-*) from the conditional prefix (cond{N}-*) so a body mixing variable-text and conditionals cannot collide (DESIGN OQ-3, proven by construction; verified by the vt-mixed scenario in slice 02).

In lowerBody, detect a .variableText segment in a .content line parallel to the existing inline-conditional detection (firstIndex(where:)); reuse inlineContinuationStatements + branchLowerer for the continuation, exactly like lowerInlineConditionalLine.

Gate: in UnsupportedConstructDetector, leadingMarker maps ONLY '~' -> 'shuffle'; '&' and '!' now return nil; the no-marker top-level-'|' path returns nil (was 'sequence'). Keep checkStatement (thread/list/random/external) and the conditional ':' discriminator untouched. The shuffle path must still throw a located, NAMED error (VT0 + S6 depend on it).

S5 reconciliation: reuse the existing committed vt-* oracle fixtures for documentedSupported rather than re-pointing the escaped reject-once (which is a latent \! sequence per U-3); the S5 supported test only compiles + builds Story (no oracle play), so any compilable variable-text fixture satisfies it.

Per ADR-025 trunk-based: RED (enable VT1, watch it fail for MISSING_FUNCTIONALITY per distill/red-classification.md) -> GREEN -> COMMIT directly to main. The pre-commit gate (.githooks/pre-commit: swiftlint --strict + swift test) must pass for the commit; do NOT --no-verify.

Confirm genuine RED before GREEN: with the gate unchanged and VT1 enabled, compileAndPlay throws CompileError (.unsupportedConstruct) — the correct MISSING_FUNCTIONALITY red, not a fixture/setup defect.

The parametrized VariableTextEmitter sequence path (OP=MIN, BOUND=S-1, no appended stage) and the shuffle-only gate already landed in slice-01 (step 01-01), so the two pure-sequence ATs are boundary-verification of already-shipped code and are expected to go GREEN immediately on enable; the vt-mixed AT is the FIRST execution of a body mixing a variable-text group (`{red|green|blue}` sequence) and an inline conditional (`{seen: Back again.|First time.}`) — it executes (not merely asserts by construction) DESIGN OQ-3: the distinct `seq{N}-*` vs `cond{N}-*` key namespaces must not collide and must play oracle-identically. Per CLAUDE.md trunk-based + the .disabled policy: remove the three `.disabled` traits (RED), confirm each enabled AT either passes on the already-landed lowering OR — only for vt-mixed if a real key-collision/continuation defect surfaces — fails for MISSING_FUNCTIONALITY (genuine RED) and is fixed inside the Compiler codegen (VariableTextEmitter / RuntimeObjectEmitter), then GREEN, then COMMIT directly to main. Do NOT modify Engine/Decoder/Facade. The full swift test suite + SwiftLint --strict pre-commit gate must pass; no --no-verify.

The sign glows blue.'], native == oracle, with NO stage-container key collision between the conditional and the sequence

(% , stageCount) : (MIN, stageCount-1)`, InkParser.modeAndContent already maps a leading `&` to .cycle, and UnsupportedConstructDetector.leadingMarker already returns nil for `&` (only `~` shuffle rejects). The two VT3 ATs are therefore boundary-verification of already-shipped code and are expected to go GREEN immediately on enable (mirroring slice-02's pure-sequence cases) — legitimate verification of the wrap-vs-clamp terminal rule across 2 stages and >2 stages (the modulo off-by-one risk at the wrap boundary, OQ-1), NOT testing-theater. Per CLAUDE.md trunk-based + the .disabled policy: confirm honest RED first (enable both ATs and run the suite BEFORE any code change; per distill/red-classification.md the expected RED mode is MISSING_FUNCTIONALITY — record if instead they are already GREEN on the slice-01 lowering), then — only if a genuine wrap/continuation defect surfaces — fix inside Compiler/Codegen (VariableTextEmitter / RuntimeObjectEmitter) only, then GREEN, then COMMIT directly to main. Do NOT modify Engine/Decoder/Facade. Do NOT touch the uncommitted Compiler_S4_CeilingTests.swift edit (belongs to slice-04, stays .disabled and unstaged). The full swift test suite + SwiftLint --strict pre-commit gate must pass; no --no-verify.

phases.name: Compiler: `not` unary operator in conditions (TheIntercept enabler — GAP 1)

steps.name: Support the `not` unary prefix operator in compiler expressions (lower to native `!`)
steps.description: Add `not <operand>` support to the compiler's Pratt expression parser so conditions like `{not think: ...}` and `{not x}` compile. Discovered by the slice-04 RED finding (the descope premise was falsified): TheIntercept uses `not` in ~50 conditions and the expression parser throws `unexpectedToken` because it has no prefix-operator path. The runtime ALREADY supports unary logical-not as the native function `"!"`. Lowering (verified vs inklecate, fixture `slice-bug-glue-after-choice.ink.json`): `not x` pushes the operand then emits `.nativeFunction("!")` (postfix). Minimal approach (per scoping): in `InkParserExpressions.parseOperand`, detect a leading `not` word token and parse it as `.functionCall(name: "!", arguments: [<operand>])`, reusing the existing function-call lowering in `RuntimeObjectEmitter.lowerFunctionCall` which already emits `.nativeFunction(oper)` — no AST change, no tokenizer change (`not` already lexes as a word). Per CLAUDE.md OOP + trunk-based: RED (author a focused failing test — a unit/integration test that compiles a `{not x}` / `{not flag: a|b}` condition and asserts the emitted runtime objects contain `.nativeFunction("!")` after the operand, OR an execution test against an existing committed `not`-using fixture if one plays) → GREEN (minimal parser branch) → COMMIT to main. Do NOT modify Engine/Decoder/Facade. Confirm no regression: the `!` already-supported path and all conditional suites stay GREEN.
acceptance_criteria: `{not x}` and `{not flag: text}` compile without throwing; the emitted runtime objects push the operand then emit `.nativeFunction("!")` (matching inklecate's postfix-`!` shape verified in slice-bug-glue-after-choice.ink.json)
A focused failing test authored in RED (compiler-level: assert emitted nodes for `not x`, or execution-level if a committed `not` fixture exists) passes GREEN after the parser branch lands
`not (a == b)` (parenthesised / compound operand) and double `not not x` lower correctly via recursion (operand is itself parsed by parseOperand)
No regression: all existing conditional / expression suites (S3, S4 ceiling, Milestone5 tier-3) stay GREEN; the full swift test suite is GREEN
Compiler-only diff under Sources/SwiftInkRuntime/Compiler/ (+ its test); no Engine/Decoder/Facade change; SwiftLint --strict R1/R3/R5 passes
steps.criteria: A focused `not`-unary test passes GREEN; emitted nodes match inklecate's push-operand-then-`!` shape; no regression in any conditional/expression suite; full swift test GREEN; SwiftLint --strict passes; no Engine/Decoder/Facade change.
steps.scenario_name: Compiler enabler — `not` unary operator (GAP 1 for TheIntercept e2e)
quality_gates: Focused `not`-unary test passes (emitted `.nativeFunction("!")` after operand)
No regression in conditional/expression suites
Full swift test suite GREEN
SwiftLint --strict --no-cache passes (R1/R3/R5)
implementation_notes: Scoping ground truth (Explore, 2026-06-15): root cause is `InkParserExpressions.parseOperand` (~lines 64-74) having no prefix path — `not` falls through to `operand()` and is mis-read as a variable reference, then the next token (`think`/`x`) has no binary operator and throws `unexpectedToken`. Fix: branch on a leading `not` word token, recursively parse the operand, return `.functionCall(name: "!", arguments: [operand])`.

Reuse: `RuntimeObjectEmitter.lowerFunctionCall` (~lines 214-231) already emits `.nativeFunction(oper)`; `not`→`!` needs no new codegen. Confirm the runtime's `!` handler exists (Decoder/InkDecoder.swift nativeFunctions set includes `!`).
Tokenizer needs NO change — `not` lexes as a word already; the `.` word-char rule (GAP 2) is separate.

Edge cases to cover: `not` immediately before a parenthesised expr, before a function call, before a dotted read-count operand (interacts with 06-01 — order: land 05-01 first, then 06-01 builds on the same operand path).

Author the RED test in Compiler_S4_CeilingTests.swift (or a focused new compiler test file under Acceptance/ if cleaner) — but if a NEW test file is created it must be listed here; prefer extending an existing compiler test. The crafter authors this focused test because no DISTILL AT exists for this internal mechanism (the port-level AT is the TheIntercept e2e, 04-01).
Per ADR-025 trunk-based: RED -> GREEN -> COMMIT to main; pre-commit gate (swiftlint --strict + swift test) must pass, no --no-verify. Trailers: Step-Id: 05-01 + Task-Id: compiler-variable-text.
deliverables: A focused `not`-unary test (in Compiler_S4_CeilingTests.swift or a new focused compiler test)

phases.name: Compiler: dotted read-count addressing of named targets in conditions (TheIntercept enabler — GAP 2)

steps.name: Resolve dotted read-count references (knot.label / knot.stitch) to a runtime path and emit .readCount
steps.description: Add compiler support for read-count references that address a NAMED target via a dotted path inside a condition, e.g. `{harris_demands_component.cant_talk_right: helplessly}`. Discovered by the slice-04 RED finding. Today the compiler treats `a.b` as a single variable-reference identifier (the tokenizer includes `.` as a word char) and emits `.variableReference("a.b")`, which the runtime cannot resolve — inklecate instead lowers it to a read-count node (`CNT?`) addressing the resolved container path. CRITICAL CORRECTNESS NOTE: the gate is hermetic EXECUTION-equivalence (native playback == committed inklecate oracle playback through the same pure-Swift `Story`), NOT byte-identical JSON — so the native compiler must emit a read-count node whose target path resolves, in the NATIVE compiler's OWN emitted container tree, to the correct named target's read count. It does NOT need to reproduce inklecate's literal path string (e.g. `harris_demands_component.0.g-1.c-14`); it needs semantic equivalence on playback. Approach (per scoping): in `RuntimeObjectEmitter.lowerExpression`'s `.variableReference` case, detect an identifier that names a container target (contains `.` and/or resolves to a knot/stitch/weave-label rather than a variable/constant), resolve it against the lowered knot/stitch/label structure to the native container path, and emit `.readCount(resolvedPath)` instead of `.variableReference`. Reuse the knot/stitch traversal already in `emitKnot`/`readKnot` and the existing divert-target path-resolution logic (diverts already resolve `-> knot.stitch`). FIRST sub-task for the crafter: determine whether the named target `cant_talk_right` is a stitch or a WEAVE LABEL `(cant_talk_right)`/gather label, and whether the compiler already parses + names weave labels; if weave-label naming is missing, that is part of this step's scope (extend the parser/emitter to register labelled gathers/choices in named content so they can be addressed). Per CLAUDE.md OOP + trunk-based: RED (author a focused failing test that compiles a small in-source `knot { = stitch ... }` or labelled-weave fixture using a dotted read-count condition and asserts emitted `.readCount` + correct playback, OR drive it via the TheIntercept compile getting past this construct) → GREEN (path resolver + emit) → COMMIT to main. Do NOT modify Engine/Decoder/Facade. If, during investigation, GAP 2 proves substantially larger than the M-L estimate (e.g. weave-label support is broadly missing across the compiler), STOP and report back to the orchestrator with the expanded scope rather than committing a partial/again-blocked state.
acceptance_criteria: A condition using a dotted read-count reference to a named target (knot.stitch or knot.label), e.g. `{some_knot.some_label: text}`, compiles and emits a read-count node (`.readCount(path)`) whose path resolves in the native emitted tree to that target's read count — NOT a `.variableReference`
On playback, the native read-count value is semantically identical to the committed inklecate oracle for the same construct (execution-equivalence, not JSON-identity)
If weave-label addressing was missing, labelled gathers/choices are now registered in named content and addressable by read-count reference (scoped sub-capability)
A focused failing test authored in RED passes GREEN; single-name read-count references (e.g. `{knot: ...}`) are unaffected or also supported as needed
No regression: all existing suites stay GREEN; the full swift test suite is GREEN
Compiler-only diff under Sources/SwiftInkRuntime/Compiler/ (+ its test); no Engine/Decoder/Facade change; SwiftLint --strict R1/R3/R5 passes
steps.criteria: A focused dotted-read-count test passes GREEN with correct emitted `.readCount` + playback-equivalent value; weave-label addressing supported if it was the gap; no regression; full swift test GREEN; SwiftLint --strict passes; no Engine/Decoder/Facade change.

implementation_notes: Scoping ground truth (Explore, 2026-06-15): the difficulty is RESOLUTION, not emission. `RuntimeObjectEmitter.lowerExpression` `.variableReference` case currently always emits `.variableReference`; it must instead recognise container-path targets and emit `.readCount(resolvedPath)` (NodeKind already has `.readCount(String)`). The oracle path `harris_demands_component.0.g-1.c-14` shows `cant_talk_right` resolves to a deeply nested labelled container (likely a weave label inside the knot's first stitch), so this is read-count addressing of a WEAVE LABEL, not a top-level stitch.

Reuse: knot/stitch traversal in `emitKnot` (~376-392) / `readKnot` (~305-334) builds the named-content tree; divert-target resolution already maps `knot.stitch` source names to paths (diverts carry the target verbatim and the runtime resolves) — but read-counts must be resolved AT COMPILE TIME. Extend `LoweringContext` (~57-70) to carry a name→path table built during lowering so `lowerExpression` can resolve.

Execution-equivalence freedom: emit the NATIVE compiler's own resolved path; do not chase inklecate's literal container IDs. The test asserts playback equality, so as long as the native runtime tree resolves the emitted path to the correct target's read count, byte differences in the path string are irrelevant.

FIRST: confirm whether the native compiler parses/names weave labels `(label)`. If not, that sub-capability is in scope here. If it balloons (labels needed broadly, multi-level nesting resolution), STOP and report expanded scope to the orchestrator before committing.
Order dependency: land AFTER 05-01 (`not`) since both touch the expression operand/lowering path; build on the same `lowerExpression` site.

The crafter authors the focused RED test (no DISTILL AT for this internal mechanism; the port-level AT is the TheIntercept e2e, 04-01). If a new compiler test file is created, list it in files_to_modify.
Per ADR-025 trunk-based: RED -> GREEN -> COMMIT to main; pre-commit gate (swiftlint --strict + swift test) must pass, no --no-verify. Trailers: Step-Id: 06-01 + Task-Id: compiler-variable-text.
